intro.title: Training, consultancy & systems change
intro.description: I support schools, colleges and organisations to create emotionally safe, relational and trauma-informed cultures where children, young people and adults can thrive.

quotedParagraph: I am particularly interested in how systems either strengthen connection and belonging, or unintentionally contribute to social thinning — where stress, exclusion and repeated negative experiences gradually reduce opportunities for trust, safety and support.

services.title: Training
services.hook: I deliver training that participants regularly describe as both deeply reflective and genuinely practical.
services.description: My sessions bring together neuroscience, psychology and trauma-informed practice with strategies that staff can apply in the classroom, the corridor, the pastoral meeting and beyond.

services.title: Consultancy
services.hook: Real change doesn't come from a report left on a shelf.
services.description: My consultancy work is collaborative and relational — I work alongside leaders and teams to explore culture, relationships, behaviour, belonging and emotional safety across the organisation.

services.title: Speaker
services.hook: My speaking blends research, storytelling and neuroscience.
services.description: I help audiences think differently about behaviour, vulnerability, inclusion and human connection — grounded, emotionally resonant and practically useful.

services.title: Systems Change
services.hook: Training alone rarely changes a system.
services.description: I support trusts, colleges, local authorities and organisations to embed trauma-informed and relational approaches across whole systems — from governance to classroom.

services.title: Supervision
services.description: Alongside my own work, I collaborate with a trusted network of experienced school-based therapists who offer individual and group supervision tailored to schools, colleges and organisations.

extra.name: Leigh Fenton
extra.description: Clinical Supervisor, Counsellor and Trainer with over 16 years' experience. Trainer and Clinical Supervisor for TISCUK, holding a PGCert in Clinical Supervision (Derby University) and PGDip in Integrative Counselling (University of Bristol).
